exemplar.title: The Relationship Between the Model Minority Stereotype and Psychological Distress in Asian Americans

detailedExemplarData.title: The Relationship Between the Model Minority Stereotype and Psychological Distress in Asian Americans

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, focused topic and sustained purpose throughout the investigation
Precisely worded research question closely tied to discussion
Logical framing in the introduction with each section linked back to focus
weaknesses: Minor lapses in conciseness and occasional repetition
Methodology only implicit with a narrow range of non-scholarly sources
Inconsistent citation practice and lack of explicit scope/delimitation statement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates its topic and purpose effectively and sustains focus (band 5) and formulates a clear, well-focused research question (band 5). However, the methodology is only partially developed with a narrow source range and no explicit analytical framework (band 3). Holistically, this merits a 5/6: strong in communication and question formulation but held back by methodological limitations.

strengths: Solid subject knowledge with effective use of quotations to support analysis
Strong contextual background situating poems within Duffyâs feminist project
Mostly accurate use of literary terminology in context
weaknesses: Reliance on non-academic websites limits depth of understanding
Technical terms sometimes implicit rather than explicitly defined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good knowledge of the primary text and applies sources coherently (top of band 3â4), but occasional inaccuracies and over-reliance on non-scholarly commentary prevent reaching excellence. Terminology use is mostly accurate but not consistently deep. A holistic score of 4/6 reflects solid performance with room for more rigorous sourcing and explicit definitions.

strengths: Logical progression and coherent overall argument structure
Effective close analysis linking poetic techniques to themes of agency and oppression
Comparative discussion across poems demonstrating analytical breadth
weaknesses: Critical evaluation of alternative interpretations remains superficial
Research draws largely on introductory secondary sources rather than scholarly articles
Limited reflection on methodological limitations in the conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay moves beyond description to offer clear analysis (band 5â6) and maintains relevance of research materials (band 4â6), but evaluation is only partially critical (band 4â6). Taken together, performance sits at the lower end of the 7â9 band for Critical Thinking. A score of 7/12 rewards the strong analysis and coherent argument while accounting for the need for deeper evaluation and more rigorous sources.

strengths: Appropriate overall structure with introduction, body sections, conclusion and Works Cited
Clear section headings and paragraphing guiding the reader
Presence of title and word count at the start
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting (missing page numbers, incomplete MLA citations)
Abrupt page breaks and lack of abstract or roadmap
Redundancy in the concluding section and uneven sub-heading use
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets basic structural conventions and includes required formatting elements, placing it within the 3â4 band. However, multiple formatting inconsistencies and organizational lapses (missing page numbers, no abstract, inconsistent MLA) limit the work to a 3/4. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects these strengths and shortcomings.

strandSelections.reasoning: Literary terminology such as âenjambment,â âextended metaphor,â âsibilance,â and âcaesuraâ is used mostly accurately and in context. Nevertheless, definitions are sometimes implicit rather than explicit, and technical concepts (e.g., âirregular line breaksâ) are not always linked to deeper interpretive insight. The consistency is good but not flawless, warranting a mid-band score of 3.

Basic layout elements are present: word count, title, and a Works Cited. However, page numbers are not visible in the provided excerpt, some citations are incomplete, and MLA formatting is inconsistent. These shortcomings fit the âsome considerations missing or incorrectâ descriptor, justifying 2 marks.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of the primary text and applies quotations effectively to illustrate points about imagery, metaphor, and structure. Source material, though modest, is generally integrated to support claims. Occasional factual slips (e.g., âMacmillaâ instead of Macmillan) and reliance on non-academic websites keep the work from the excellence band, but overall understanding is clear and coherent, justifying the top of the 3-4 band.

The methodology is mostly implicit: close textual analysis supplemented by secondary commentary sites. While the chosen poems are well-justified, the range of supporting sources is narrow (blogs, LitCharts, GCSE sites) and lacks scholarly depth. Citations are inconsistent, and no specific analytical framework is described. These limitations indicate a partially complete but not fully developed methodology, meriting the middle band and a score of 3.

The student states a clear, focused topic â the exploration of female agency and patriarchal oppression in Carol Ann Duffyâs The Worldâs Wife â and sustains this purpose throughout the essay. The introduction frames the investigation logically, and each body section explicitly links back to the overarching focus on transformation and resistance. Minor lapses in concision and occasional repetition prevent the work from feeling fully âeffective,â hence the lower end of the top band (5 rather than 6).

Research is adequate: it includes primary textual evidence and a handful of secondary commentaries that are directly relevant to the question. However, most sources are introductory in nature; academic journal articles or theoretical lenses are absent. This places the work at the lower end of the 4-6 band â reasonable relevance but not âmostlyâ or âconsistentlyâ strong â so a 4 is assigned.

The research question is precisely worded, appropriately narrow for a literary investigation, and tightly connected to the ensuing discussion. Each analytical section references the key terms of the question (agency, transformation, patriarchal oppression). The formulation is clearly focused, though not exceptionally innovative, so a score of 5 is most fitting.

The structure is appropriate for an EE: clear sectioning, introduction, conclusion, and Works Cited. Headings and paragraphing guide the reader effectively. Minor organisational issues (e.g., abrupt page-break markers, lack of abstract) prevent the full 4 marks, but expectations are broadly met.

The essay follows a logical progression (introduction, poem-by-poem discussion, conclusion) and offers a reasoned overall argument. Evaluation of differing interpretations or the limitations of sources is brief, and alternative readings are seldom weighed. Consequently, the discussion is âpartially criticalâ rather than fully critical, meriting a 4.

The analysis moves beyond description, linking textual techniques to themes of agency and oppression and drawing comparisons across poems. Arguments are generally supported with quoted evidence, though some points remain surface-level and counter-arguments are limited. This represents clear but not yet sophisticated analysis, aligning with a mid-band mark of 5.

annotations.comment: The evaluation remains largely descriptive; deepen critical evaluation by weighing alternative interpretations of Medusaâs transformation.
The comparative discussion of Thetis effectively ties poetic devices to themes of resistance, maintaining focus on the research question.
Reflect on the limitations of your methodology in the conclusion to demonstrate critical self-evaluation.
Explicitly state the scope and delimitations of the study in your methodology to clarify boundaries of your investigation.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, effectively guiding the investigation from the outset.
The student provides a word count at the start, meeting the formatting requirement.
There is no distinct title before the research question; include a concise title to clarify the focus of the investigation.
Consider previewing your research methodology briefly in the introduction to orient the reader to your analytical approach.
The opening paragraph on page 2 provides strong contextual background and situates the poems within Duffyâs broader feminist project.
Citation of âDoug, et a, 2024â is inconsistent and incomplete; correct author spelling and ensure complete citation details.
Define technical terms such as âextended metaphorâ and âenjambmentâ explicitly upon first use to reinforce conceptual understanding.
Analysis of Little Red Cap carefully links textual evidence to themes, demonstrating an effective analytical approach.
Relying on LitCharts for thematic interpretation limits academic rigor; integrate peer-reviewed or scholarly sources for deeper analysis.
Introduce clear subheadings when shifting from Little Red Cap to Medusa to improve organization and reader guidance.
The in-depth imagery analysis in the Medusa section demonstrates clear subject knowledge and appropriate use of literary terminology.
Incorporate a named theoretical lens (e.g., feminist criticism) to frame your analysis methodology and strengthen academic rigor.
The analysis of sibilance and consonance would benefit from clearer ties to the overarching theme of female agency.
Break the long sections into shorter paragraphs and add subheadings for each poem to enhance readability and structure.
The conclusion synthesizes findings well and reiterates how each poem contributes to understanding female agency.
The concluding section repeats earlier points; refine to avoid redundancy and ensure each sentence adds new insight.
Use complete in-text citations (e.g., author and page number) rather than website names to strengthen academic integrity.
A dedicated reflection section is missing; add analytical or evaluative reflection to satisfy the engagement criterion.
The publisher name âMacmillaâ is misspelled in the Works Cited; correct it to âMacmillan.â
Works Cited entries are inconsistently formatted; apply MLA conventions uniformly (italics, punctuation, author order).
Alphabetize your Works Cited list according to MLA rules to ensure correct ordering of sources.
Including access dates and URLs for online sources shows good formatting practice for digital references.
Page numbers are not visible in the header or footer; add numbering to support navigation and meet formatting standards.
Introduce a brief roadmap of the reportâs structure after the introduction to improve overall organization.
Clarify how irregular line breaks in Medusa connect to your argument rather than just describing the poetic device.
When discussing Thetis, link each shapeshifting example explicitly back to the research question to maintain focus.
Provide deeper evaluation of how childbirth in Thetis both reinforces and subverts patriarchal norms.
